channel.title: Communication on Chatrix.One
channel.description: Recent content in Communication on Chatrix.One

item.title: A Bulgarian XMPP Server
item.description:
Chatrix.One is a Bulgarian XMPP server for free, end-to-end encrypted communication, with no telemetry and no ads.

Features

  • Instant messaging with reactions and emoji support
  • Image and file sharing of any type
  • Location sharing
  • Audio and video calls
  • Voice messages
  • Group chat
  • Sent, delivered and read indicators
  • Contact avatars
  • Desktop sync
  • Compatible with all major operating systems

Why Chatrix.One?

  • Built on the proven XMPP standard
  • End-to-end encryption with OMEMO or OpenPGP
  • Encrypted audio and video calls (DTLS-SRTP)
  • RESPECTS and VALUES your privacy
  • Does NOT require personal data to register
  • Does NOT serve ads, banners or offers
  • Does NOT require consent to unreasonable policies
  • Runs on Ejabberd - an open source platform
  • 100% compliance with available XEPs1
  • Decentralised architecture
  • Server located in Switzerland, accessible worldwide
  • Powered 100% by renewable energy!

Instructions on how to use the service, FAQ, rules and privacy policy are available at the official documentation page: docs.chatrix.one

item.title: XMPP - Communicate Freely!
item.description:
XMPP (also known as Jabber) is a well-established instant messaging protocol used by millions every day, often without them realising it. Various popular messaging apps started out as XMPP chat clients before becoming something isolated, shiny and proprietary.

There are many reasons you might consider chatting through an XMPP client instead of joining the latest trending platform.

What is XMPP?

XMPP, short for Extensible Messaging and Presence Protocol, is a decentralised messaging standard similar to email. Anyone can create an XMPP account on one server and chat with someone registered on a completely different server. Like email, no single company has access to or control over all users’ data globally. You can choose from different XMPP providers, just as you can choose between different email providers.
